# Parallel-CLI Contract

> Status: **PROPOSED** — partition stub. D2 (`m55-d2-parallel-cli-substrate`) promotes to STABLE during execute.
> Version: 0.1.0 (stub) → 1.0.0 (STABLE target)
> Owner: D2
> Consumer: D5 (verify-gate Track 2)

## Purpose

N-worker pool runner. Every spawn flows through `bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs::captureSpawn`. Tee log streams per worker. Lifecycle/timeout/fail-fast policy. Engine-only — does NOT touch command files in M55.

## Library API (target)

```js
const { runParallel } = require('./bin/parallel-cli.cjs');
const result = await runParallel({
  workers: [{ id: 'tsc', cmd: 'npx', args: ['tsc', '--noEmit'], timeoutMs: 60000 }, ...],
  maxConcurrency: 4,        // required; no implicit default
  failFast: false,
  teeDir: '.gsd-t/verify-gate/{runId}/'
});
// result: { ok, schemaVersion, results: [{id, ok, exitCode, durationMs, stdoutPath, stderrPath, signal?}], wallClockMs }
```

## CLI Form (target)

```
node bin/parallel-cli.cjs --plan plan.json --max-concurrency 4 --json
echo '{"workers":[...]}' | node bin/parallel-cli.cjs --max-concurrency 2 --json
```

## Calibration via D3 Map

Default `maxConcurrency` is **not** baked into D2. D5 reads `.gsd-t/ratelimit-map.json::recommended.peakConcurrency` (or D3's `summary.declaredSafe` cells) at wire-in time and passes the value. Without the map, D5 falls back to `maxConcurrency=2`.

## captureSpawn Invariant

Every worker spawn flows through `captureSpawn`. For deterministic CLIs (no LLM call), `usage` envelope is absent and the token-log row renders `—` per the canonical pattern. D2 NEVER fabricates `0` tokens.

## Fail-Fast Policy (target)

`failFast: true` → first non-zero exit cancels in-flight workers via `SIGTERM`, escalates to `SIGKILL` after 5s grace.

## Promotion to STABLE

D2 promotes to v1.0.0 STABLE when:
- Library + tee helper + proof CLI shipped
- ≥6 unit tests pass
- `bin/m55-substrate-proof.cjs` demonstrates ≥3× wall-clock speedup (charter SC2)

These 3-4 failures are **pre-existing flakes** unrelated to D2. They were present before any of my changes. So my D2 introduces ZERO new failures... except my own throttling test, which is timing-flaky.

# GSD-T Progress

## Project: GSD-T Framework (@tekyzinc/gsd-t)
## Status: ACTIVE — M55 PARTITIONED (5 file-disjoint domains, Wave 1 D1+D3+D4 parallel, Wave 2 D2, Wave 3 D5)
## Date: 2026-05-09
## Version: 3.24.10

## Current Milestone

M55 — CLI-Preflight Pattern + Parallel-CLI Substrate + Rate-Limit Map + Context Briefs + Verify Gate (PARTITIONED, MERGED scope)

## Milestones

| # | Milestone | Status | Version | Domains |
|---|-----------|--------|---------|---------|
| M55 | CLI-Preflight Pattern + Parallel-CLI Substrate + Rate-Limit Map + Context Briefs + Verify Gate (MERGED scope) | PARTITIONED | 3.24.10 → 3.25.10 (planned) | 5 file-disjoint domains. Wave 1 (parallel): D1+D3+D4. Wave 2: D2. Wave 3: D5. **D1 State-Precondition Library** — `bin/cli-preflight.cjs` (pluggable check registry: branch-guard, ports-free, deps-installed, contracts-stable, manifest-fresh, working-tree-state; deterministic `{ok,checks[],notes[]}` envelope; schema-versioned, zero deps mirroring `bin/parallelism-report.cjs`). **D2 Parallel-CLI Substrate** — `bin/parallel-cli.cjs` (N-worker pool, every spawn flows through `captureSpawn`, log-stream tee to per-worker NDJSON, lifecycle/timeout/fail-fast policy, engine-only — does NOT touch command files yet; concurrency defaults calibrated from D3 empirical map). **D3 Rate-Limit Probe + Empirical Map** — `bin/gsd-t-ratelimit-probe.cjs` (synthetic-worker harness mirroring real GSD-T spawn shape; sweep matrix `{1,2,3,4,5,6,8} workers × {10k,30k,60k,100k} context`; captures per-worker time-to-first-token, 429 count + which worker, total wall-clock, effective ITPM/OTPM; backoff probe + 5-min steady-state probe; real spawns into throwaway worktree; output `.gsd-t/ratelimit-map.json`). **D4 Context-Brief Generator** — `bin/gsd-t-context-brief.cjs` (single entry: `gsd-t brief --kind {execute|verify|qa|red-team|design-verify|scan} --domain X --json`; produces `.gsd-t/briefs/{spawn-id}.json` ~2k JSON snapshot replacing 30–60k context re-read per worker; `context-brief-contract.md` v1.0.0 — schema, freshness via mtime hash-stamp, fail-open vs fail-closed, idempotent-join rule). **D5 Verify-Gate + Wire-In + Doc Ripple** — `bin/gsd-t-verify-gate.cjs` (two-track gate consuming both: state-preflight envelope from D1 hard-fails on non-`ok` checks, AND parallel CLI fan-out via D2 substrate runs typecheck+lint+tests+dead-code+secrets+complexity using off-the-shelf CLIs (tsc/biome/ruff/knip/gitleaks/scc/lizard) with ≤500-token JSON summary; LLM judges summary, never raw output); wire preflight into `gsd-t-execute` Step 1 + verify-gate into `gsd-t-verify` Step 2; update qa/red-team/design-verify subagent prompts with brief-first rule; doc ripple to architecture.md, requirements.md, CLAUDE.md, gsd-t-help.md; Pre-Commit Gate update. **Goal**: lift practical parallelism ceiling from ~3 LLM workers to ~6–10 mixed workers (1 LLM judge + N CLIs) by replacing deterministic LLM work with deterministic CLI work, prove via empirical measurement, and gate every spawn with deterministic state checks. **Why**: pain pattern across M48/M49/M50/M52 — silent-skip regressions caught only by retrofit hooks, ad-hoc `Promise.all([Task,...])` patterns bypassing `bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs`, "verify" PASS without precondition checks; PLUS hitting undocumented Claude rate-limit ceiling at ~3 parallel workers because every worker re-loads ~60k context blowing through ITPM. **Success criteria** (8 falsifiable, measure before tag/publish per `feedback_measure_dont_claim.md`): (1) state-preflight envelope schema published as STABLE contract; (2) substrate proves ≥3× speedup on a real fan-out scenario via in-tree proof CLI (measurement, not claim); (3) verify-gate blocks ≥3 distinct state-preflight failure classes (wrong-branch, port-conflict, contract-DRAFT) in `e2e/journeys/`-shape tests with manifest entries; (4) ≥40% token reduction per milestone for execute+verify cycles vs trailing-3 baseline (M52, M54, prior); (5) zero 429 errors at parallelism level D3 declared safe + peak parallelism ≥6 concurrent workers; (6) verify-gate wall-clock ≤ ½ trailing-3 median; (7) Red Team GRUDGING PASS — ≥5 broken patches, all caught (preflight-skip-on-error, parallel-substrate-bypasses-capture, verify-gate-falsy-true, branch-guard-typo, contract-staleness-ignored, brief-staleness-ignored); (8) zero regressions on `npm test` (baseline 2262/2262 unit + 1 documented env-bleed). **Out of scope**: rewriting all 55 command files to call the substrate directly (separate ratchet milestone, pending M55 ship); - 2026-05-07 11:51: [success] [debug] Fixed M53b conversation-capture project-routing under parallel Claude Code sessions. Root cause: `scripts/hooks/gsd-t-conversation-capture.js::_resolveProjectDir(payload)` fell through to `_walkUpForProject(process.cwd())` when `GSD_T_PROJECT_DIR` was unset and `payload.cwd` was absent. `process.cwd()` for a Claude Code Stop/UserPromptSubmit hook is the directory the user launched `claude` from. When two parallel Claude Code sessions ran in different projects (`/Users/david/projects/GSD-T` and `/Users/david/projects/Move-Zoom-Recordings-to-GDrive`), the SAME node-runtime hook process resolved to whichever project the hook inherited via cwd — frames cross-talked. Concrete misroute: GSD-T orchestrator's NDJSON written into `Move-Zoom-Recordings-to-GDrive/.gsd-t/transcripts/in-session-800d4b3b-….ndjson` (verified 2026-05-07 11:30 — content was THIS session's banners, file was the wrong project's transcripts dir). **Fix** (2 new private fns + reordered `_resolveProjectDir`): `_slugFromTranscriptPath` extracts the directory-name slug from `~/.claude/projects/{slug}/{sid}.jsonl`; rejects paths outside that root. `_slugToProjectDir` DFS-walks the filesystem from `/`, greedily consuming `-`-separated tokens as directory names; first leaf where `.gsd-t/` exists wins. Disambiguates literal-hyphen project names (`Move-Zoom-Recordings-to-GDrive`) by consulting the disk — disk is the oracle. Rejects slugs containing `..`, `/`, `\\`, `\0`, or not starting with `-`. New priority: env → transcript_path slug → `payload.cwd` → cwd walk-up.
